# License Privileges Reference

*Safety-critical. Do not edit without fetching primary sources.*
*Sources: §97.301, §97.305, §97.313 — https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/47/97.301*
*Table verified 2026-06-11 against Cornell LII mirror of CFR.*

---

## KO6NOI — Technician Class

### HF Privileges

| Band | Freq Range | Modes | Power Limit | FT8 Freq | FT8 Legal? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 80m | 3.525–3.600 MHz | CW + Data/Digital | 200W PEP | 3.573 MHz | **YES** (within segment) |
| 40m | 7.025–7.125 MHz | CW + Data/Digital | 200W PEP | 7.074 MHz | **YES** (within segment) |
| 15m | 21.025–21.200 MHz | CW + Data/Digital | 200W PEP | 21.074 MHz | **YES** (within segment) |
| 10m | 28.000–28.500 MHz | ALL | 200W PEP | 28.074 MHz | YES |

### VHF/UHF Privileges
Full privileges on all VHF/UHF bands — no restrictions by license class.

### Key Facts (verified against Part 97)
- Power limit on HF: **200W PEP** on all Technician segments (§97.313(b)) — NOT 28W
- 10m upper edge: **28.500 MHz** (not 29.700) — data to 28.300, phone to 28.500
- Emission types: §97.305 does NOT restrict Technician to CW-only on HF segments.
  **CW AND data/digital (FT8, FT4, RTTY) are both legal on all Technician HF bands.**
- Technician CAN legally run FT8 on 40m (7.074 MHz is within 7.025–7.125 MHz)

---

## General Class Privileges (for reference / upgrade path)

| Band | Freq Range | Modes | Power |
|---|---|---|---|
| 80m | 3.525–4.000 MHz | ALL | 1500W |
| 40m | 7.025–7.300 MHz* | ALL | 1500W |
| 20m | 14.025–14.350 MHz* | ALL | 1500W |
| 17m | 18.068–18.168 MHz | ALL | 1500W |
| 15m | 21.025–21.450 MHz | ALL | 1500W |
| 12m | 24.890–24.990 MHz | ALL | 1500W |
| 10m | 28.000–29.700 MHz | ALL | 1500W |

*Gaps where Extra-only segments exist:*
- 80m: 3.600–3.800 MHz is Extra-exclusive (General has phone from 3.800–4.000)
- 40m: 7.125–7.175 MHz is Extra-exclusive (General jumps to 7.175 for phone)
- 20m: 14.150–14.225 MHz is Extra-exclusive

### What Opens Up at General (upgrade nudge)
- 20m FT8 at 14.074 MHz — the most active DX band in the world
- 40m phone (7.175–7.300 MHz) — major nets and DX phone contacts
- 80m full phone segment
- Full 10m (to 29.700 MHz)
- Significant increase in DX opportunity across all HF bands

---

## FT8 Frequencies by Band

| Band | FT8 Freq | Technician Legal? |
|---|---|---|
| 160m | 1.840 MHz | No |
| 80m | 3.573 MHz | Yes (CW/Data segment) |
| 40m | 7.074 MHz | Yes (CW/Data segment) |
| 30m | 10.136 MHz | No |
| 20m | 14.074 MHz | No (General/Extra only) |
| 17m | 18.100 MHz | No |
| 15m | 21.074 MHz | Yes (CW/Data segment) |
| 12m | 24.915 MHz | No |
| 10m | 28.074 MHz | Yes (full 10m privileges) |

---

## Primary Sources

- §97.301 (authorized frequency bands): https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/47/97.301
- §97.305 (authorized emission types): https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/47/97.305
- §97.313 (power limits): https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/47/97.313
- ARRL Technician privileges: https://www.arrl.org/what-privileges-does-a-technician-have
- FCC Part 97 full text: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-47/chapter-I/subchapter-D/part-97

**Do not edit this table without fetching the primary sources above. A wrong entry means
advising an operator to transmit out of band — an FCC violation.**
